one_line_ask: From the approved folk-art 20th-Anniversary master, produce a print-ready 5-screen separation pack (white underbase + four spot-colour halftone plates with a dot-gain choke), a vectorized hard-edge lettering/line plate, a grain-textured client proof, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template, and a Firefly board of the campus reference photos for the screen printer.
brief:
John Paul the Great Academy (a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana) has APPROVED the final folk-art / festival-poster illustration celebrating the school's first twenty years — a handcrafted, story-driven composition weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks, rich with discoverable details. The illustration phase is complete and signed off; this engagement is the screen-print PRODUCTION phase. You are given: (1) the approved master artwork as a high-resolution flattened RGB PNG (~24x36 in @300dpi, full-bleed), in which the figurative folk-art scene carries soft tonal passages and the commemorative title/year lettering is hard-edge solid; (2) the campus reference photos that accompanied the creative brief (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse facade, founding-class photo); and (3) the print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet template (separations.ai) carrying real artboards, registration marks, and ink-name slots.

Deliver a 5-screen spot-colour separation pack for a manual screen print on natural/cream stock: a WHITE UNDERBASE plate (solid fill behind all art), and four spot-ink plates — Marian Blue, Antique Gol
